user_query:
I’m an aspiring young archer, and I track my practice in a small script. I want confidence that my stats are computed correctly and that the status message is concise and encouraging (I love the supportive vibe of Christopher Davis). Please do the following using the provided files and produce the requested outputs under the specified paths:

- Write a small, focused test suite (pytest) in tests/ that validates app/archery_stats.py against the data in input/sessions.jsonl. The tests should:
  - Independently compute expected aggregates from input/sessions.jsonl inside the tests (don’t hardcode numbers) and assert that summarize_sessions returns matching values for: total_ends, total_arrows, avg_score (rounded to 1 decimal), best_end, and per-distance ends and average scores.
  - Validate that format_status(summary) returns a single-line message of 120 characters or less that contains the avg_score and best_end values and at least one distance average (e.g., “18m avg 54.0”).
  - Include an edge-case test that calls summarize_sessions([]) and asserts zeros where appropriate; and that format_status on an empty/zero summary returns a brief, friendly message indicating no sessions yet (120 characters or less).
- Update app/archery_stats.py only as needed to satisfy the above tests, keeping function names the same. In particular, rewrite the status text generation so that format_status returns a concise, supportive line in plain English that mentions the key stats.
- Rewrite the three draft messages in input/messages.md for tone, clarity, and brevity. Produce out/messages_rewritten.md with each draft reduced to a single line (one line per draft), each ≤120 characters, warm and supportive (in the spirit of a calm coach), preserving intent and any important details (practice, competition, missed session). Do not copy the original wording—make it concise.
- Run the script from the command line to generate a structured summary file: python app/archery_stats.py input/sessions.jsonl out/summary.json. The output JSON must include keys: total_ends, total_arrows, avg_score, best_end, and distances (mapping of distance in meters to {"ends", "avg"}).
- Run the test suite from the command line and save a structured test report: produce out/test-results.xml in JUnit XML format (e.g., via pytest’s built-in JUnit XML output). Ensure tests pass.

Deliverables to produce:
- tests/ (with your pytest tests)
- out/summary.json (written by running the script)
- out/test-results.xml (written by running the tests)
- out/messages_rewritten.md (your concise rewrites, one line per draft)

Notes:
- Keep outputs deterministic and derived from the provided input files.
- The requested files and their paths are required for verification. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
